alignments.pub_claim_text: The homogeneous ensemble HAT made up of 10 models achieved a detection rate of 97.2%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed intrusion detection system is efficient and effective in dealing with concept drift while considering the resource constraints of Wireless Sensor Networks.

alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same conclusion about effectiveness on concept drift and consideration of WSN resource constraints with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ensemble models have received less attention in streaming applications despite being useful in offline learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate this limitation/observation about the relative attention to ensembles in streaming applications.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Wireless sensor networks (WSN) are vulnerable to security breaches due to their harsh deployment conditions and constrained resources.
alignments.rationale: This background/context claim appears in the publication but has no corresponding statement in the preprint claims list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An intelligent and efficient intrusion detection system can be developed using ensemble learning to improve detection performance in WSN applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a high-level contribution statement about ensemble learning in WSNs that is not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PL optimization with the REINFORCE algorithm can be used for optimizing fairness measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel distribution representation called acPPG improves over acPL for optimizing fairness metrics for queries with one session.
alignments.rationale: The publication renames/reframes the methods (acPL/acPPG) and focuses on the improved distribution representation; the underlying statement about PL-style optimization is made more specific under new naming.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPG improves over PL for optimizing fairness metrics for queries with one session.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel distribution representation called acPPG improves over acPL for optimizing fairness metrics for queries with one session.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative performance claim but uses the specific method names acPPG and acPL, clarifying terminology while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of PPG in fairness optimization is significantly boosted when accurate utility estimations are available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When accurate utility estimations are available, the performance of acPPG in fairness optimization is significantly boosted compared to lower quality utility estimations from a learning to rank model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical dependence on utility estimation quality, adding a specific comparison context but not altering the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pairwise probabilities in PPG allow for the imposition of pairwise constraints to optimize fairness metrics and control ranking performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pairwise probabilities in acPPG allow for the imposition of pairwise constraints to optimize both fairness metrics and ranking performance.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim the same capability of pairwise probabilities to impose constraints affecting fairness and ranking; the publication uses the acPPG naming convention.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BILP-Q reformulates the CSGP in terms of a Quadratic Binary Combinatorial Optimization (QUBO) problem.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Morphing attacks cannot be assessed by traditional risk assessment criteria used for other types of attacks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ML-based semantic communication system SINFONY achieves a tremendous rate-normalized SNR shift up to 20 dB compared to classically designed communication systems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach extends the fundamental modeling of semantics to the complete communications Markov chain using hidden random variables.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedRN helps mitigate the gap between low- and high-performance clients by training only with a selected set of clean examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedRN mitigates the gap between low- and high-performance clients by training only with a selected set of clean examples.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication raises the claim's certainty (label changed to high) compared to the preprint's medium-certainty presentation, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The superiority of FedRN is demonstrated via extensive evaluations on three real-world or synthetic benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedRN significantly improves the test accuracy in the presence of noisy labels.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized demonstration via extensive evaluations and datasets; the publication focuses on the performance result (accuracy improvement) without referencing the evaluation scope, narrowing and revising the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairSR can outperform state-of-the-art SR models in recommendation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairSR can outperform state-of-the-art SR models in recommendation performance.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are textually identical and convey the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recommended items by FairSR exhibit promising interaction fairness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recommended items by FairSR exhibit promising interaction fairness.
alignments.rationale: The claim about interaction fairness is unchanged between preprint and publication in wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new metric, interaction fairness, is defined to estimate how recommended items are fairly interacted by users with different protected attribute groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new metric, interaction fairness, is defined to estimate how recommended items are fairly interacted by users with different protected attribute groups.
alignments.rationale: Both versions introduce the same metric with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairSR incorporates the knowledge of users' and items' attributes and their correlation into entity representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairSR incorporates the knowledge of users’ and items’ attributes and their correlation into entity representations.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both documents (only minor typographic difference).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMATO outperformed widely used DR techniques in preserving the global structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMATO outperformed widely used dimensionality reduction techniques in preserving the global structure of high-dimensional data.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the domain explicit (high-dimensional data) while retaining the same result; this clarifies and slightly narrows the claimed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMATO produced competitive accuracy in representing the local structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMATO produced competitive accuracy in representing the local structure of high-dimensional data.
alignments.rationale: Same performance claim but the publication explicitly ties local-structure accuracy to high-dimensional data, a clarifying/narrowing detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMATO is preferable in terms of robustness over diverse initialization methods, number of epochs, and subsampling techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMATO is preferable in terms of robustness over diverse initialization methods, numbers of epochs, and subsampling techniques.
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical; wording differences are minor (pluralization) and do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised learning can be an effective tool for designing near-optimal feedback controllers for high-dimensional nonlinear dynamic systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised learning can be an effective tool for designing near-optimal feedback controllers for high-dimensional nonlinear dynamic systems.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some neural networks with high test accuracy can fail to even locally stabilize the dynamic system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some neural networks with high test accuracy can fail to even locally stabilize the dynamic system.
alignments.rationale: The result about high-accuracy networks failing to stabilize is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network architectures guarantee local asymptotic stability while retaining the approximation capacity to learn the optimal feedback policy semi-globally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network architectures guarantee local asymptotic stability while retaining the approximation capacity to learn the optimal feedback policy semi-globally.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Standard neural networks can fail to stabilize the dynamics even when trained well.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Standard neural networks can fail to stabilize the dynamics even when trained well.
alignments.rationale: The statement about standard networks failing to stabilize is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architectures are always at least locally stabilizing and can achieve near-optimal performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architectures are always at least locally stabilizing and can achieve near-optimal performance.
alignments.rationale: The result about local stabilization and near-optimal performance is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general duality result for Wasserstein distributionally robust optimization holds for any Kantorovich transport cost, measurable loss function, and nominal probability distribution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general duality result for Wasserstein distributionally robust optimization applies to any Kantorovich transport cost, measurable loss function, and nominal probability distribution.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same general duality result with equivalent scope and conditions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interchangeability principle holds if and only if certain measurable projection and weak measurable selection conditions are satisfied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interchangeability principle holds if and only if certain measurable projection and weak measurable selection conditions are satisfied.
alignments.rationale: The iff statement about measurable projection and weak measurable selection conditions is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach provides a rigorous treatment of duality results in distributionally robust Markov decision processes and distributionally robust multistage stochastic programming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Zhang, Yang, and Gao present a novel elementary proof that shortens existing frameworks and offers surprising generalizations for Wasserstein distributionally robust optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution emphasizing a novel elementary proof and broader generalizations; this clarifies and amplifies the earlier claim about rigorous treatment and extensions to specific application domains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis is extended to other problems such as infinity-Wasserstein distributionally robust optimization, risk-averse optimization, and globalized distributionally robust counterpart.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong duality in Wasserstein distributionally robust optimization is contingent on a certain interchangeability principle.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the role of interchangeability for strong duality; this is related to the preprint's claim about extensions and additional problem classes but reframes the emphasis toward theoretical conditions, so the mapping is plausible though less direct.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DIRA produces state-of-the-art performance in comparison with other methods from the literature.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work shows the possibility of governing equation discovery of physical problems that contain multiple and uncertain processes using deep learning and data assimilation methods.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of learning part-whole hierarchies and compositionality but omits explicit dataset names and one-shot transfer specifics, making the claim less dataset-specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNPs provide an intuitive and explainable way of composing objects and scenes, allowing rich compositionality and intuitive interpretations of objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNPs can learn part-whole hierarchies for a variety of image datasets, allowing rich compositionality and intuitive parts-based explanations of objects.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about compositionality and intuitive, parts-based explanations.

alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the call for systematic study, with similar emphasis on understanding attack success.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a taxonomy for attack and defence approaches in model stealing.

alignments.rationale: The taxonomy contribution is present and phrased equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide guidelines on selecting the right attack or defence strategy based on the goal and available resources.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this specific claim about providing actionable guidelines; instead it adds a claim about current attack strategies reducing defence effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate a clear change in slope around 15 km, independently confirming the conclusions of Singer et al. (2019).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate a clear change in slope around 15 km, independently confirming the conclusions of Singer et al. (2019).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The slopes found in this study are slightly flatter than those reported by Robbins and Singer (2021).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The slopes found in this study are slightly flatter than those reported by Robbins and Singer (2021).

alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same contribution claim with only minor wording variation ('sequences' vs 'series').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate that KitBit can predict the following terms in the largest number of series to date.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate that KitBit can predict the following terms in the largest number of series to date.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is conserved exactly between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can effectively capture the relationship between picosecond laser machining parameters and the desired cuts on industrial-grade alumina ceramic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can effectively capture the relationship between picosecond laser machining parameters and the desired cuts on industrial-grade alumina ceramic.
alignments.rationale: The core conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural Networks are the most efficient models for predicting the outputs of laser machining based on complex correlations between laser parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural Networks are the most efficient models for predicting the outputs of laser machining parameters.
alignments.rationale: Publication shortens the phrasing by dropping the explicit mention of 'complex correlations' but retains the same comparative claim about neural networks' efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of an ML model to predict input parameters for achieving target channel geometry significantly reduces the cost and effort of experimental laser machining.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of an ML model to predict input parameters for achieving target channel geometry significantly reduces the cost and effort of experimental laser machining.
alignments.rationale: The text of the claim is the same but the publication lowers the stated certainty from high to medium, indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed techniques can be applied to a wide range of ceramic laser machining processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed techniques can be applied to a wide range of ceramic laser machining processes.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved identically in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mixed-precision quantization provides excellent efficiency gains with limited accuracy drops.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mixed-precision quantization provides excellent efficiency gains with limited accuracy drops.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication about mixed-precision benefits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed novel NAS selects the bit-width of each weight tensor channel independently, allowing for higher precision assignment to the most informative features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed novel NAS selects the bit-width of each weight tensor channel independently, allowing for higher precision assignment to the most informative features.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about channel-wise bit-width selection is restated verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our networks reduce the memory and energy for inference by up to 63% and 27% respectively compared to a layer-wise approach, for the same accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our networks reduce the memory and energy for inference by up to 63% and 27% respectively compared to a layer-wise approach, for the same accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Performance numbers and comparative claim are identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MADDi classifies MCI, AD, and controls with 96.88% accuracy on a held-out test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MADDi classifies MCI, AD, and controls with 96.88% accuracy on a held-out test set.
alignments.rationale: Exact performance claim preserved between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of cross-modal attention with self-attention performed the best in the model's evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of cross-modal attention with self-attention performed the best among different attention schemes.
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies the comparison context (among attention schemes) making the claim more specific while keeping its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Any multimodal mixture of input features without access to structured clinical information suffered marked performance losses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Any multimodal mixture of input features without access to structured clinical information suffered marked performance losses.
alignments.rationale: The observation about performance losses is retained verbatim; only the publication reclassifies it as a conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the merit of combining multiple input modalities via cross-modal attention to deliver highly accurate AD diagnostic decision support.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the merit of combining multiple input modalities via cross-modal attention to deliver highly accurate AD diagnostic decision support.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Domain knowledge does not improve machine learning performance in most tasks tested, with band gap prediction being the notable exception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Domain knowledge does not improve machine learning performance in most tasks tested, with band gap prediction being the notable exception.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication downgraded the stated certainty to medium, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The radial basis function improves the linear separability of chemical datasets in all 10 datasets tested.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The radial basis function improves the linear separability of chemical datasets in all 10 datasets tested.
alignments.rationale: The empirical result about RBF improving separability is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A framework for the application of the radial basis function in the LOCO-CV process can improve the outcome of LOCO-CV measurements regardless of machine learning algorithm, choice of metric, and choice of compound representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kernelised LOCO-CV provides a framework for improving the outcome of LOCO-CV measurements regardless of machine learning algorithm, choice of metric, and choice of compound representation.
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the same framework as 'Kernelised LOCO-CV' but preserves the scope and intended improvement, so this is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kernelised LOCO-CV is recommended as a training paradigm for measuring the extrapolatory power of an algorithm on materials data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kernelised LOCO-CV can measure the extrapolatory power of an algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the prescriptive recommendation (removes explicit recommendation) and instead states a capability; wording also has higher certainty (high vs medium), so the claim is revised and narrowed in scope with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-objective hyperparameter optimization is essential for optimizing machine learning pipelines beyond just predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-objective hyperparameter optimization is essential for optimizing machine learning pipelines beyond just predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of knowledge and readily available software implementations hinders the practice of multi-objective hyperparameter optimization. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of knowledge and readily available software implementations hinders the practice of multi-objective hyperparameter optimization. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is carried forward unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work provides an extensive survey of existing optimization strategies, including evolutionary algorithms and Bayesian optimization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work provides an extensive survey of existing optimization strategies from evolutionary algorithms and Bayesian optimization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution; wording is virtually identical and the scope remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utility of multi-objective optimization is illustrated in various machine learning applications considering multiple objectives. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-objective optimization can improve various aspects of machine learning applications, including fairness, interpretability, and robustness. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives specific examples of benefits (fairness, interpretability, robustness), thereby clarifying and narrowing the originally broader claim and reducing stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML models developed on the HASDM and CHAMP datasets show evidence of post-storm cooling in the middle-thermosphere. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HASDM-ML and CHAMP-ML show evidence of post-storm cooling indicating that this phenomenon is present in the original data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about post-storm cooling in the same datasets with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NRLMSIS 2.0 and JB2008-ML do not account for post-storm cooling and perform poorly following strong geomagnetic storms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NRLMSIS 2.0 and JB2008-ML do not account for post-storm cooling and perform poorly in periods following strong geomagnetic storms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same evaluation of the baseline models and their shortcomings with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Density reductions of up to 40% can occur 1--3 days post-storm depending on location and the strength of the storm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Density reductions up to 40% can occur 1–3 days post-storm depending on the location and strength of the storm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and conditions are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The value function polytope for finite state-action discounted Markov decision processes is characterized using a hyperplane arrangement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The value function polytope for finite discounted Markov decision processes is characterized using a hyperplane arrangement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core theoretical characterization as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Geometric Policy Iteration (GPI) is proposed as a new algorithm to solve discounted MDPs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Geometric Policy Iteration (GPI) is proposed as a new algorithm to solve discounted MDPs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The algorithm proposal is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPI updates the policy of a single state by switching to an action that is mapped to the boundary of the value function polytope. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPI updates the policy of a single state by switching to an action that is mapped to the boundary of the value function polytope. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mechanism of the GPI update is described identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The complexity of GPI achieves the best known bound of policy iteration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complexity of GPI achieves the best known bound O|𝓐|over 1 - γ log 1 over 1-γ of policy iteration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the explicit form of the previously stated 'best known bound', clarifying the claim without changing its scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPI allows asynchronous updates of state values, providing more flexibility compared to traditional policy iteration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPI allows asynchronous updates of state values, providing more flexibility compared to traditional policy iteration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about asynchronous updates and flexibility is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DSCA could outperform existing state-of-the-art methods in cancer prognosis, by an average C-Index improvement of around 4.6%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dual-stream network with cross-attention (DSCA) outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in cancer prognosis, achieving an average C-Index improvement of around 4.6%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported performance improvement and its magnitude are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DSCA network is more efficient in computation, having more learnable parameters but less computational costs compared to a typical existing multi-resolution network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DSCA network is more efficient in computation, having more learnable parameters (6.31M vs. 860.18K) but lower computational costs (2.51G vs. 4.94G) compared to a typical existing multi-resolution network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds concrete parameter and FLOP numbers, clarifying the original efficiency claim without changing its scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The key components of DSCA, dual-stream and cross-attention, contribute to the model's performance, gaining an average C-Index rise of around 2.0% while maintaining a relatively-small computational load. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The key components of DSCA, dual-stream and cross-attention, contribute to the model’s performance, resulting in an average C-Index rise of around 2.0% while maintaining a relatively small computational load. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about component contributions is preserved, but the publication increases the stated certainty and frames it as a result rather than a medium-confidence conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DSCA could serve as an alternative and effective tool for WSI-based cancer prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DSCA could serve as an alternative and effective tool for WSI-based cancer prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding recommendation and certainty about DSCA's applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CIRCA can improve the recall of the top-1 recommendation by 25% over the best baseline method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CIRCA can improve the recall of the top-1 recommendation by 25% over the best baseline method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical improvement is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CIRCA constructs a graph among monitoring metrics based on the knowledge of system architecture and a set of causal assumptions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CIRCA constructs a graph among monitoring metrics based on the knowledge of system architecture and a set of causal assumptions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution statement but lowers the expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The core idea of CIRCA is a sufficient condition for a monitoring variable to be a root cause indicator, based on the change of probability distribution conditioned on the parents in the Causal Bayesian Network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The core idea of CIRCA is a sufficient condition for a monitoring variable to be a root cause indicator, based on the change of probability distribution conditioned on the parents in the Causal Bayesian Network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The theoretical condition and its framing are preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulation study illustrates the theoretical reliability of CIRCA. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulation study illustrates the theoretical reliability of CIRCA. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The simulation-based validation claim appears in the publication with the same emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CIRCA is formulated as a new causal inference task named intervention recognition. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This formulation as a named task is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Echo state networks with randomly generated internal weights can approximate any continuous causal time-invariant operators under certain conditions on the activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Echo state networks (ESNs) with randomly generated internal weights can approximate any continuous causal time-invariant operators under certain conditions on the activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same technical universality result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The universality of echo state networks holds for general activation functions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The universality of ESNs holds for general activation functions, not just ReLU. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same general claim about activation functions, only adding the explicit contrast to ReLU but not changing meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An explicit construction for sampling procedures of internal weights is provided for ReLU activation in echo state networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An explicit construction for sampling procedures of internal weights in ESNs is provided for ReLU activation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same constructive contribution for ReLU ESNs with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approximation error of the constructed ReLU echo state networks for sufficiently regular operators is quantified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation error of the constructed ReLU ESNs for sufficiently regular operators is quantified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The error-quantification result is preserved verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning a pre-trained language model often suffers from overfitting, leading to poor generalizability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning a pretrained language model often suffers from overfitting, leading to poor generalizability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same diagnosis about fine-tuning overfitting with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Layerwise Noise Stability Regularization (LNSR) framework effectively addresses the overfitting problem. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through both theoretical and experimental analyses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication not only asserts effectiveness but explicitly adds theoretical validation and experiments, clarifying and strengthening the original contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates advantages over other state-of-the-art algorithms including L2-SP, Mixout, and SMART. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed layerwise noise stability regularization (LNSR) method outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified on question answering tasks, which are more difficult than previous text classification tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention evaluation specifically on question answering tasks or the comparative difficulty claim, so this specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm enhances in-domain performance and improves domain generalization performance on out-of-domain data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The empirical results indicate that the proposed method can improve the ability of language models to domain generalization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports improved domain generalization consistent with the preprint's empirical claim; wording differs slightly but the substantive claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design process of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) is tedious and relies heavily on human intuition and trial-and-error. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design approach of deep neural networks (DNNs) is tedious and relies heavily on human intuition and experience. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same critique of manual network design, with minor terminology changes (ANNs vs DNNs) that do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continual adaptiveness and automation of neural networks are crucial for domains with limited model accessibility after deployment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continual adaptiveness and automation of neural networks are crucial for domains with limited model accessibility after deployment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This core conclusion is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study formalizes the Continually-Adaptive Neural Networks (CANNs) paradigm and outlines research directions for lifelong autonomous ANNs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study conducts the first extensive review on the intersection between neural architecture search (NAS) and continual learning (CL). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution from a broad formalization of a CANNs paradigm to a focused review on NAS×CL, a substantive change in content and narrower scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accessible models of ANNs require frequent maintenance post-deployment to address issues like Concept/Data Drift. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models are generally bound to their training contexts and do not consider their surrounding environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses a closely related but slightly more general observation about models being tied to training contexts versus the preprint's emphasis on maintenance needs; this reads as a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Universal adversarial perturbations (UAP) play a critical role in Embodied Vision Navigation due to their computation efficiency and application practicality during attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Universal adversarial perturbations (UAP) play a critical role in Embodied Vision Navigation due to their computation efficiency and application practicality during attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level claim about the importance of UAPs unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing UAP methods are sub-optimal as they ignore the system dynamics of Embodied Vision Navigation. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing UAP methods are sub-optimal as they ignore the system dynamics of Embodied Vision Navigation. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same limitation claim but expresses it with higher certainty, increasing confidence in the statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Consistent Attack methods, Reward UAP and Trajectory UAP, effectively consider the dynamics of the Markov Decision Process and significantly drop the performance of various victim models in the PointGoal task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Consistent Attack methods, Reward UAP and Trajectory UAP, effectively consider the dynamics of the Markov Decision Process and significantly drop the performance of various victim models in the PointGoal task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution and empirical effect on PointGoal performance are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results indicate serious potential risks for applying Embodied Vision Navigation methods to the real world. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results indicate serious potential risks for applying Embodied Vision Navigation methods to the real world. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about real-world risks remains but the publication expresses it with reduced certainty, tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Gaussian Mixture Scope (GMS) effectively regularizes the chaotic scale distribution in crowd localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gaussian Mixture Scope (GMS) effectively regularizes the chaotic scale distribution in crowd localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same high-level effectiveness claim for GMS.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GMS approach leads to overfitting due to the dislodging of hard samples in the training set. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GMS approach can lead to overfitting due to the dislodging of hard samples in the training set. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about overfitting caused by dislodging hard samples appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Scoped Teacher facilitates knowledge transfer between the GMS and the model, addressing the overfitting issue. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a Scoped Teacher facilitates the transfer of latent knowledge from data to model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the Scoped Teacher's role as transferring latent knowledge from data to model; this clarifies the mechanism though the contribution is aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance in crowd localization as measured by F1-measure across five datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance in crowd localization as measured by F1-measure across four datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Notable performance degradation is experimentally observed for both one-stage and two-stage detectors when noise is introduced to the bounding box annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noticeable performance degradation is experimentally observed for both one-stage and two-stage detectors when noise is introduced to the bounding box annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same experimental observation with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthesized noise results in performance decrease from 38.9% AP to 33.6% AP for FCOS detector on COCO test split. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthesized noise results in performance decrease from 38.9% AP to 33.6% AP for FCOS detector on COCO test split. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and context are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A self-correction technique based on a Bayesian filter for prediction ensemble is proposed to better exploit the noisy location annotations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A self-correction technique based on a Bayesian filter for prediction ensemble is proposed to better exploit the noisy location annotations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments consistently demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, increasing the degraded performance of the FCOS detector from 33.6% AP to 35.6% AP on COCO. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method increases the degraded performance of the FCOS detector from 33.6% AP to 35.6% AP on COCO. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the numeric improvement claim; wording is slightly rephrased but meaning and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained on incomplete imaging data segmented lesions well, often equivalently to those trained on complete data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained on incomplete data can segment lesions very well, often equivalently to those trained on the full complement of images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incomplete data segmentation models could accurately detect enhancing tumour in the absence of contrast imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Segmentation models can accurately detect enhancing tumour in the absence of contrast-enhancing imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Segmentation models can quantify enhancing tumour without the administration of intravenous contrast. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This suggests translation to clinical practice, where incomplete data is common, may be easier than hitherto believed. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work substantially extends the translational opportunity for quantitative analysis to clinical situations where the full complement of sequences is not available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach provides semi-automatic support to model optimization based on the combined assessment of model complexity and fitness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach provides semi-automatic support to model optimization based on the combined assessment of model complexity and fitness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A model simplification approach is proposed, which abstracts the raw model at the desired granularity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution statement with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of meta-states can potentially simplify the model and enhance its interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of meta-states can potentially simplify the model and improve its interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about meta-states is preserved with equivalent nuance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technological solution demonstrates its capabilities using three datasets from different applications in the healthcare domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The capabilities of the technological solution are demonstrated using three datasets from different applications in the healthcare domain. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The case study provides insights on better practices for improving interpretability and the complexity/fitness balance in process models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The case study provides insights on better practices in improving interpretability and complexity/fitness balance in process models. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised learning techniques were implemented to classify the Galaxy Zoo DECaLS dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised learning techniques were implemented to classify the Galaxy Zoo DECaLS dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best results for clustering were produced by the agglomerate clustering method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best results for clustering were produced by the agglomerate clustering method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The clustering result is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase in performance of agglomerate clustering compared to k-means clustering was not significant considering the increase in clustering time. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in performance of agglomerate clustering compared to k-means clustering was not significant considering the increase in clustering time. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance/time trade-off claim is carried forward intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After optimizations, the unsupervised learning approach could serve as a basis for generating more 'human-like' galaxy morphology classifications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: After optimizations, the unsupervised learning approach could serve as a basis for generating more 'human-like' galaxy morphology classifications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The tentative conclusion about future potential remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach defines meaningful and consistent concepts in an existing engineering dataset by partitioning the complete feature set into several subsets called description spaces. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach defines meaningful and consistent concepts in an existing engineering dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core contribution but omits the explicit mention of partitioning in this particular claim, splitting that detail into a separate claim (see Q1).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel concept quality measure is proposed, providing an objective value for a given definition of concepts in a dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel concept quality measure is proposed, providing an objective value for a given definition of concepts in a dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about the concept quality measure is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usefulness of the concept quality measure is demonstrated using a realistic engineering dataset consisting of about 2500 airfoil profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usefulness of the concept quality measure is demonstrated using a realistic engineering dataset of about 2500 airfoil profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration claim including dataset size and context is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The numerical optimization procedure maximizes the concept quality measure and finds meaningful concepts for different setups of the description spaces while incorporating user preference. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not include an explicit statement about a numerical optimization procedure that maximizes the measure; that specific procedural claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified concepts can be used to select archetypal representatives of the dataset that exhibit characteristic features of each concept. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the selection of archetypal representatives of the dataset that exhibit characteristic features of each concept. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the archetype-selection conclusion but states it with higher certainty/strength than the preprint did.
